helpful: The complete petition and appendix exposed both the textual argument and the absence of a direct circuit split.
Fee-class, originating-circuit, relist, and CVSG base rates supported calibrated certiorari odds.
gaps: CourtListener MCP was temporarily unavailable because its daily rate limit was exhausted.
The corpus query surface cannot search SCOTUS priors by legal issue, so its returned grant-side priors were only general context.
notes: The provisioned record was sufficient for a prediction despite the live-research degradation. A provisioned response brief would have improved adversarial assessment, but the docket reflects that respondents waived response.
